content: To separate gravitational deflection from refraction in your prism experiment, model the beam’s path using the combined metric of the prism’s refractive index and the Earth’s weak gravitational field, then calculate the tiny additional angular deviation from the gravitational potential gradient along the beam path and subtract it from the total measured deflection. In practice, this involves adding the gravitational potential as a perturbation to the refractive index profile and solving the ray equations accordingly.
